dashboard.label: Ping Monitor Setup
dashboard.description: Configure Ping Monitor settings - run this once after installation

div.p: Configure your Splunk index and sourcetype settings below.

search.query: | inputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup | search _key="global" | head 1 | eval status=if(configured=="true", "✅ Configured", "⚠️ Not Configured") | eval index=coalesce(index, "(not set)") | eval sourcetype=coalesce(sourcetype, "(not set)") | eval metrics_index=coalesce(metrics_index, "(not set)") | table status, index, sourcetype, metrics_index | rename status as "Status", index as "Events Index", sourcetype as "Sourcetype", metrics_index as "Metrics Index" | append [| makeresults | eval Status="⚠️ Not Configured - Please save configuration below", "Events Index"="(default: ping)", "Sourcetype"="(default: ping_monitor)", "Metrics Index"="(default: ping_metrics)" | head 1] | head 1

div.p: 💡 Instructions: Enter your index and sourcetype values above, then click the Submit button at the top right of the form to save your configuration.

search.query: | makeresults | eval _key="global", index="$config_index$", sourcetype="$config_sourcetype$", metrics_index="$config_metrics_index$", configured="true", last_updated=strftime(now(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | outputlookup ping_monitor_settings_lookup append=false key_field=_key | eval result="✅ Configuration saved! Index: $config_index$, Sourcetype: $config_sourcetype$, Metrics Index: $config_metrics_index$" | table result | rename result as "Status"
index="$config_index$" sourcetype="$config_sourcetype$" | stats count | eval count=if(isnull(count), 0, count)
| mstats count(_value) WHERE index="$config_metrics_index$" metric_name="ping.*" | eval count=if(isnull(count), 0, count)

| inputlookup ping_health_lookup.csv | stats count as records, max(_time) as last_updated | eval status=if(records>0, "✅ Health lookup populated with ".records." records", "⚠️ Health lookup is empty - run the Update Health Lookup saved search") | eval last_updated=if(isnotnull(last_updated), strftime(last_updated, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "Never") | table status, records, last_updated | rename status as "Status", records as "Records", last_updated as "Last Updated" | append [| makeresults | eval Status="⚠️ Health lookup does not exist yet", Records=0, "Last Updated"="Never" | head 1] | head 1

html.p: Click Submit above to also generate/refresh the health lookup table for asset correlation features.

search.query: index="$config_index$" sourcetype="$config_sourcetype$" record_type=summary | stats latest(packet_loss_pct) as packet_loss_pct, latest(avg_latency_ms) as avg_latency_ms, latest(target_ip) as target_ip, latest(group) as group, latest(entitytype) as entitytype by hostname | eval health_status = case( packet_loss_pct == 0, "Healthy", packet_loss_pct > 0 AND packet_loss_pct < 50, "Degraded", packet_loss_pct >= 50 AND packet_loss_pct < 100, "Critical", packet_loss_pct == 100, "Down", true(), "Unknown" ) | outputlookup ping_health_lookup.csv | stats count as updated_records | eval result="✅ Health lookup updated with " . updated_records . " records" | table result | rename result as "Status"

ol.li: Make sure your ping monitor script is running and sending data to Splunk HEC
Click Submit above to save your configuration
Navigate to Ping Monitor Overview to view your monitoring dashboard
Optionally enable the scheduled saved search "Update Health Lookup" in Settings → Saved Searches
